description:
You are an observability engineer doing some spring-cleaning of the existing Grafana dashboards that live under
  “/home/user/dashboards”.  Any dashboard whose file-name contains the exact substring “_legacy” has to be
  removed from the active pool and parked inside “/home/user/dashboards/archive”.

  1. The starting folder structure is:

     /home/user/dashboards/
       ├─ prometheus_k8s_legacy.json
       ├─ grafana_overview_legacy.json
       ├─ service_latency.json
       └─ readme.md

  2. Required end-state:
     • A new directory called “archive” must exist directly inside “/home/user/dashboards”.
     • Both “prometheus_k8s_legacy.json” and “grafana_overview_legacy.json” must reside **inside** that
       “archive” directory and must no longer appear in the top-level “/home/user/dashboards”.
     • “service_latency.json” and “readme.md” must stay exactly where they are.
     • A plain-text log file called “/home/user/dashboard_cleanup.log” must be created that lists each file
       that was moved.  The file must contain one line per moved file, using the *exact* format shown below,
       and the lines must be sorted in lexicographic order by their source path:

         /home/user/dashboards/grafana_overview_legacy.json >> /home/user/dashboards/archive/grafana_overview_legacy.json
         /home/user/dashboards/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json >> /home/user/dashboards/archive/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json

       ‑ Each line begins with the absolute source path, followed by one space,
         the two greater-than symbols “>>”, another single space, and then the absolute destination path.
       ‑ No leading or trailing spaces are allowed.
       ‑ The file must end with a single newline character.

  3. Constraints & hints:
     • Accomplish the move operation *only* with one find command and one xargs-driven move
       (plus, optionally, one small command to create the “archive” directory).
     • Do not interactively prompt the user.
     • After running your commands, it should be possible for an automated test to verify:
         – the final directory tree,
         – the presence and correctness of “dashboard_cleanup.log”,
         – that the files’ contents remain unchanged during the move.
     • You can assume the starting directory tree already exists exactly as shown above.
truth:
<!--   Ground-truth used by the automated checker.  DO NOT reveal any of this in the task description.   -->

  <!-- Files that exist before the agent starts -->
  <initial_state>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json">
      {"title":"Prometheus K8s Legacy","version":1}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/grafana_overview_legacy.json">
      {"title":"Grafana Overview Legacy","version":1}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/service_latency.json">
      {"title":"Service Latency","version":2}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/readme.md">
      These dashboards are managed by the observability team.
    </file>
  </initial_state>

  <!-- Expected files and their exact contents after the agent finishes -->
  <final_state>
    <directory path="/home/user/dashboards/archive"/>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/archive/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json">
      {"title":"Prometheus K8s Legacy","version":1}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/archive/grafana_overview_legacy.json">
      {"title":"Grafana Overview Legacy","version":1}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/service_latency.json">
      {"title":"Service Latency","version":2}
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboards/readme.md">
      These dashboards are managed by the observability team.
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/dashboard_cleanup.log">
      /home/user/dashboards/grafana_overview_legacy.json >> /home/user/dashboards/archive/grafana_overview_legacy.json
      /home/user/dashboards/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json >> /home/user/dashboards/archive/prometheus_k8s_legacy.json
    </file>
  </final_state>
